nodes.label: AI as coding assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI handling open-ended or esoteric code tasks
nodes.grounding_span: if I've broken the task down enough there's not much refining to do, but if I keep the scope quite open then I do find I need to correct things

nodes.label: AI as professional polish tool
nodes.grounding_span: general smartening up documents using Gemini to refine grammar and also identify areas of improvement

nodes.label: AI as rapid prototyping tool for business needs
nodes.grounding_span: I wrote a whole 'who's on holiday per client project' system in a day, purely by using AI prompts

nodes.label: AI trustworthiness and verification
nodes.label_negative: Organizational paranoia about AI data handling
nodes.grounding_span: being able to get companies to trust where their data is going is important

nodes.label: AI as learning tool for better coding patterns
nodes.label_negative: AI as crutch that degrades coding skills
nodes.grounding_span: I still learn from the code output, things like map/reduce/filter are different to how I'd code, so the code is nicer

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: it'll increase how productive I can be outside of work

nodes.label: Skill development and learning
nodes.grounding_span: I still learn from the code output

nodes.label: Organizational trust and accountability
nodes.grounding_span: being able to get companies to trust where their data is going is important

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for GUI prototyping
nodes.grounding_span: that made me very satisfied, and also solved a business need

nodes.label: Frustration with AI overstepping scope
nodes.grounding_span: if I keep the scope quite open then I do find I need to correct things or ask for help fixing bugs

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I think I'll use it more and more

nodes.label: Trust in AI data security despite organizational pushback
nodes.grounding_span: I suggested we should create our own shared drive in the office and mail server if we really didn't trust google...and they saw my thought process there and realised they were being paranoid

nodes.label: Task decomposition into small verifiable steps
nodes.grounding_span: Each step was small and a few times I had to retry but on the whole was good

nodes.label: Pragmatic advocacy — uses evidence to shift organizational norms
nodes.grounding_span: I played a big part pushing for this too as I've found it useful in my own tests
